description_long: This is a traditional log cabin with a rectangular footprint, featuring a dark shingled gable roof and four prominent brick chimneys, two on each end. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontally laid logs, giving it a rustic appearance, while the window and door frames are painted in a contrasting red. The front facade includes a covered porch with wooden steps and railings, flanked by two large windows. The side elevations show symmetrical window placement and the ends of the log walls, with the gable ends clad in red-painted wood. The rear of the cabin mirrors the front but lacks a porch, and all windows are vertically oriented with white sashes. The overall structure suggests a sturdy, historic residential building suitable for rural or park-like urban settings.
description: A rustic log cabin with a gable roof, four brick chimneys, red-trimmed windows, and a covered front porch. The structure features horizontally laid logs and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, central entrance, two large windows, and two chimneys on each side.
description_view_1: Side gable end with two chimneys, a single centered window, and red-painted wood cladding on the gable.
description_view_2: Rear view with three windows, no porch, and the same log construction and red trim as the front.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with two chimneys, a single window, and a small covered area beneath the gable.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in dark shingles, featuring four brick chimneys at the ends
attribute_window_arrangement: Symmetrical arrangement with two large vertically oriented windows flanking the front door, three evenly spaced windows on the rear, and a single centered window on each gable end; all windows are rectangular with white sashes and red trim
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade, accessed via the porch; no other doors visible
